etymology.description: Depicts a man's face, with an eye exaggerated, derived from 𦣻; the horizontal stroke was added later
etymology.interpretation: The character 面(miàn, "face") visually represents a person's face with an exaggerated eye, highlighting the frontal aspect of the head. The design was later complemented by a horizontal stroke to complete its pictorial depiction.
etymology.simplification: In the case of 麵, the component 麥(麥) (mài, "wheat") was eliminated to simplify the character, resulting in 面 being used as the sole representation.

examples.chinese: 她的面容(她的面容)总是带着微笑(總是帶著微笑)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de miànróng zǒng shì dài zhe wéixiào.
examples.english: Her face always wears a smile.

examples.chinese: 这个湖的表面(這個湖的表面)很平静(很平靜)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège hú de biǎomiàn hěn píngjìng.
examples.english: The surface of this lake is very calm.

examples.chinese: 他面对(他面對)困难(困難)毫不退缩(毫不退縮)。
examples.pinyin: Tā miànduì kùnnán háo bù tuìsuō.
